raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I made a credit card purchase from XXXX for an item described as being in very good condition. When the item was received, it did not match the description. The handles were significantly distorted, the structure was compromised, and the item could not be opened with one hand, indicating a functional defect beyond normal wear or creasing. 
I contacted the merchant and provided photo and video evidence of the condition. In chat correspondence, the merchant acknowledged the issue, stated that a refund was issued, and later confirmed that my account was restricted due to the dispute. No refund was received. 
I filed a billing dispute with Barclays Bank Delaware on XX/XX/2025, for an item not as described. Barclays declined the dispute based on the merchants normal return or final sale policy, without addressing the merchants misrepresentation exception, the functional defect, or the evidence provided. The dispute was not evaluated under a significantly not as described standard. 
When I attempted to re-dispute and submit additional documentation, including photo and video evidence and the merchants checkout language stating that items which arrive damaged, defective, or not as described are always free to return, Barclays document upload portal malfunctioned and rejected valid files, preventing full submission of supporting documentation during the re-dispute process. 
I am requesting review of whether Barclays conducted a reasonable investigation and appropriately applied dispute standards for an item that was not as described.
